candidates.why: Revenue label but appears unpopulated in preview; may be an alternate taxonomy label.
Clear revenue total and populated, but subtotal/total is less preferred than a direct top-line sales label when available.

candidates.row_label: Gain on sales of assets held for sale
candidates.why: Non-operating gain, not revenue.
Balance sheet liability, not income statement revenue.
Best direct top-line sales label; populated for all years shown.
Balancing row, not an economic revenue line.

candidates.row_label: Comparable operating result, as % of net sales
candidates.why: Margin ratio, not revenue.

variables.notes: Used a priority list for possible label changes over time; direct top-line label preferred over total.

candidates.row_label: Materials and supplies, purchases during the period
candidates.why: Direct production input cost, but appears to be only one component / a more specific breakdown rather than the broadest available cost-of-sales proxy.
Looks like one operating expense subcomponent, not the primary COGS line.

variables.notes: Candidate list appears to miss the best COGS proxy. 'Materials' is present in the full Income Statement preview and is broader/more consistently populated than 'Materials and supplies, purchases during the period', making it the most plausible direct cost line. Manual review recommended because inventory-change mechanics could mean true cost of sales is spread across multiple rows. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Materials

candidates.why: Development capitalization is not SG&A expense; exclude from overhead.

R&D-related, but appears as supplemental disclosure far below operating result, so less suitable for SG&A inclusion.
Balance sheet asset, not SG&A expense.
Balance sheet accumulated amortization, not SG&A expense.
Balance sheet liability, not SG&A expense.

candidates.why: Capitalized amount, not current-period R&D expense.

Balance sheet asset, not R&D expense.
Balance sheet contra-asset, not R&D expense.

candidates.why: Best consistently populated finance-cost line; broader than pure interest expense but usable when no single total interest expense line is reliably reported.

variables.notes: Used a priority list. 'Financial expenses' is the most consistently reported expense line and is preferred here because no single complete interest-expense line is reliably populated across years.
